name: NAS Oceana
tagline: The Navy's East Coast master jet base in Virginia Beach, home of the Atlantic Fleet's strike fighters.
_note: Navy master jet base (Virginia Beach, Hampton Roads VA). CONUS full template (BAH MHA VA298 Norfolk/Portsmouth, which covers Virginia Beach; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Home of Atlantic Fleet strike fighters (F/A-18E/F Super Hornet). Part of the Hampton Roads Navy complex (NS Norfolk already in app); JEB Little Creek nearby. Medical: branch clinic under Naval Medical Center Portsmouth. Facts verified from official sources (cnic.navy.mil NAS Oceana, NMC Portsmouth, DeCA/NEX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Virginia is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 23454 (Virginia Beach).

data.Role: The Navy's East Coast master jet base
data.Nearby: JEB Little Creek and the larger Hampton Roads Navy complex
data.Setting: A large coastal city with Atlantic beaches

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Naval Air Station Oceana is the Navy's East Coast master jet base, home to the Atlantic Fleet's strike fighter squadrons flying the F/A-18E/F Super Hornet. Its jets are a constant presence over Virginia Beach.

It's part of the dense Hampton Roads military region, which also includes Naval Station Norfolk and JEB Little Creek, in a large coastal city known for its Atlantic beaches and an affordable-for-the-coast cost of living.

sections.title: Big-City Beach Living in Hampton Roads

data.subtitle: Virginia Beach is a large, military-friendly beach city. The main choice is balancing how close to the oceanfront you want to be against commute, schools, and price.
intro: Hampton Roads is one of the biggest military regions in the country, so the area is used to military families and offers a wide range of neighborhoods and prices. On-base housing is available; many live off base across Virginia Beach.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools, balancing the beach areas against the more suburban, often more affordable parts of the city.

recommendations.advice: Central Virginia Beach neighborhoods near Oceana keep the commute short.
The oceanfront and nearby areas put you by the Atlantic, lively and popular but busier in summer.
Southern and western Virginia Beach (and Chesapeake) offer newer homes, good schools, and more space.
On-base housing is available across the Hampton Roads complex; weigh it against the local market.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official NAS Oceana site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: NAS Oceana and the surrounding Hampton Roads complex have privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA VA298 (Norfolk/Portsmouth, covers Virginia Beach)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Virginia Beach City Public Schools serves most military families, with neighboring Chesapeake and Norfolk districts depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient and complex care via Naval Medical Center Portsmouth and the network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and in military-heavy Hampton Roads many sailors return to the area, which makes buying worth a look.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your sea-duty rotation feels uncertain.
You want time to learn the area and pick the right part of the city.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat Hampton Roads assignments, which are common.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable-for-the-coast market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the large Navy presence and repeat tours make Hampton Roads a common place to buy. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book Navy Gateway Inns & Suites or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on Virginia Beach before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Virginia vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Virginia Beach City Public Schools (or your local district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the Oceana clinic or Naval Medical Center Portsmouth.

data.a: It's the Navy's East Coast master jet base, home to Atlantic Fleet strike fighter squadrons flying the F/A-18E/F Super Hornet.

Across Virginia Beach. Central neighborhoods are close to the gate; the oceanfront is lively; southern and western Virginia Beach and Chesapeake offer more space and good schools. On-base housing is also available.

data.q: How does it relate to Norfolk?
data.a: Both are part of the Hampton Roads Navy region. Oceana is the Virginia Beach jet base; Naval Station Norfolk is the huge ship homeport nearby.
Oceana falls in MHA VA298 (Norfolk/Portsmouth, which covers Virginia Beach). Use the calculator above for your grade.
